event_claims.description: Sponsor A made unsolicited initial contact expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.
quote_texts: In February 2013, Stephen I. Sadove, Saks’ Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, received an unsolicited phone call from a representative of a private equity firm, which we refer to as Sponsor A, expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Mr. Sadove met with Richard Baker of Hudson’s Bay at Mr. Baker's request to discuss a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay.
quote_texts: On April 1, 2013, Mr. Sadove met with Richard Baker, the Director, Governor, and Chief Executive Officer of Hudson’s Bay, at the request of Mr. Baker and discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay.

event_claims.description: The board directed management to continue exploring potential transactions after discussing expressions of interest from Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A.
quote_texts: The board directed Mr. Sadove and Saks management to continue exploring these potential transactions.

event_claims.description: The board authorized implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with a potential acquiror could be reached.
quote_texts: authorized (1) the implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with one of the potential acquirors which had executed a confidentiality agreement could be reached on terms that would offer attractive value to Saks shareholders

event_claims.description: Sponsor A entered into a confidentiality agreement with Saks.
quote_texts: On April 26, 2013, Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with each of Sponsor A and Sponsor E

event_claims.description: Sponsor E entered into a confidentiality agreement with Saks.
quote_texts: On April 26, 2013, Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with each of Sponsor A and Sponsor E

event_claims.description: Hudson’s Bay entered into a confidentiality agreement with Saks.
quote_texts: On April 30, 2013, Saks and Hudson’s Bay entered into a confidentiality agreement.

event_claims.description: Sponsor G entered into a confidentiality agreement with Saks.
quote_texts: On July 8, 2013, Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor G.

event_claims.description: Hudson’s Bay submitted a proposal expressing continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
quote_texts: On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Sponsor E together with Sponsor G submitted a proposal expressing continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
quote_texts: On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Saks received a purported acquisition proposal letter from Company H.
quote_texts: On July 21, 2013, Saks received a letter from Company H, a privately held company based in the U.S. unknown to Saks and its advisors, purporting to propose to acquire Saks for an aggregate price of $2.6 billion in cash, with no details or further information.

event_claims.description: Goldman Sachs unsuccessfully attempted to contact Company H, and neither Saks nor Goldman Sachs received subsequent communications from Company H.
quote_texts: Goldman Sachs subsequently attempted on more than one occasion to contact the appropriate person at Company H both by telephone and by e-mail to discuss the purported offer further but was unsuccessful in making contact with such person. Neither Saks nor Goldman Sachs received any subsequent communications from Company H.

event_claims.description: Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that it was prepared to offer $16 per share, subject to negotiating a definitive binding agreement.
quote_texts: On July 24, 2013, representatives of Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, Hudson’s Bay was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock

event_claims.description: Sponsor A and Sponsor E did not indicate they were prepared to increase their indicated price beyond their initial range.
quote_texts: There was no indication from Sponsor A or Sponsor E that they were prepared to increase their indicated price beyond their initially indicated range of $14.50 to $15.50 per share of common stock

event_claims.description: The board authorized senior management and advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson’s Bay to determine whether an agreement could be reached.
quote_texts: The board authorized senior management and Saks’ advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson’s Bay to determine if an agreement could be reached.

event_claims.description: The board unanimously approved the merger and merger agreement and recommended shareholder approval.
quote_texts: Following extensive discussion, the board unanimously agreed to approve the merger and the merger agreement, and the transactions contemplated thereby, substantially in the form presented to the board, and to recommend the approval of the merger agreement by Saks’ shareholders.

event_claims.actor_label: Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub
event_claims.description: Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents.
quote_texts: Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents later on July 28, 2013.

event_claims.description: During the go-shop process, Goldman Sachs contacted 58 potentially interested third parties on behalf of Saks.
quote_texts: During the go shop process, Goldman Sachs, on behalf of Saks, contacted 58 potentially interested third parties, including private equity firms, companies involved in the retail industry and other potential acquirors.

event_claims.description: No contacted party in the go-shop process submitted an acquisition proposal for Saks.
quote_texts: None of the parties contacted as part of the go shop process, including Company I, has submitted an acquisition proposal for Saks.

Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E)
On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.

During the go shop process, Goldman Sachs, on behalf of Saks, contacted 58 potentially interested third parties, including private equity firms, companies involved in the retail industry and other potential acquirors.
Of those contacted, only six parties expressed interest
only one of the six (which we refer to as Company I) executed a confidentiality agreement with, and conducted a due diligence investigation of, Saks
